name: Gratitude Circle
notes:
This is a literature study and discussion meeting using conference approved literature written by women and non-binary people.

Parking lot is on Josephine Boyd Blvd. between Walker Avenue & Morton Street
Use the "Church Offices" entrance between the playground and the Forest Chapel's entrance directly off of the parking lot. Please follow the signs to the meeting room downstairs.

Childcare provided

We welcome guests of all abilities — a ramp-accessible entrance is available if you need wheelchair or special access. Please text message 646-729-8209 if you need assistance from a home group member getting into the meeting.

Our group prioritizes the safety of those in attendance, therefore, we ask that group members and others refrain from any behavior or language which might compromise another person’s safety. Our Traditions suggest that no one should have to tolerate racial, sexual orientation, gender, age or other discrimination when they seek help from A.A.

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.


Meeting Schedule:
- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection
- Thur: Chair’s Choice
- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion
- Sat: Speaker Step Discussion

name: Many Paths (Secular)
notes:
This Many Paths meeting endeavors to maintain a tradition of free expression and conduct a meeting where alcoholics may feel free to express any doubts or disbeliefs they may have and to share their own personal form of spiritual experience, their search for it, or their rejection of it. Our only wish is to ensure suffering alcoholics that they can find sobriety in AA without having to accept anyone else's beliefs or having to deny their own.

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.


Meeting Schedule:
- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection
- Thur: Chair’s Choice
- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion
- Sat: Speaker Step Discussion

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.


Meeting Schedule:
- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection
- Thur: Chair’s Choice
- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion
- Sat: Speaker Step Discussion

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.


Meeting Schedule:
- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection
- Thur: Chair’s Choice
- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion
- Sat: Speaker Step Discussion

name: Back to Basics - new series started with 'As Bill Sees It'
notes:
This meeting has started a new Series of working through the 'As Bill Sees It' book of selected writings of the A.A.'s co-founder.

Throughout the year, one or more of the following literature books will be selected: Alcoholics Anonymous (The Big Book), Twelve Steps and Twelve Traditions, Living Sober, Came to Believe, Pass it On, Dr. Bob and the Good Old Timers, Alcoholics Anonymous Comes of Age, as well as Back to Basics, A New Pair of Glasses and Alcoholics Anonymous, and Drop the Rock. We do not wish to engage in any controversy. Our sole purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety by taking the 12 Steps of Alcoholics Anonymous.

notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.

- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection

- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion

This Many Paths meeting endeavors to maintain a tradition of free expression and conduct a meeting where alcoholics may feel free to express any doubts or disbeliefs they may have and to share their own personal form of spiritual experience, their search for it, or their rejection of it. Our only wish is to ensure suffering alcoholics that they can find sobriety in AA without having to accept anyone else's beliefs or having to deny their own.

name: Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.

name: Into Action
notes:
This is a moderated literature study rotating through topics related to the three legacies (unity/traditions, recovery/steps, service).

Please follow the signs to the meeting room.

We welcome guests of all abilities — a ramp-accessible entrance is available if you need wheelchair or special access. Please text message 336-663-2881 if you need assistance from a home group member getting into the meeting.

Service animals welcome.

Our group prioritizes the safety of those in attendance, therefore, we ask that group members and others refrain from any behavior or language which might compromise another person’s safety. Our Traditions suggest that no one should have to tolerate racial, sexual orientation, gender, age or other discrimination when they seek help from A.A.

name: Zoom Progress Not Perfection - Women's Meeting
Zoom Progress Not Perfection - Women's Meeting
Unity and Belonging
notes:
Unity & Belonging is an open online AA meeting focused on the LGBTQ+ community and our allies. All alcoholics are welcome! Meetings are held daily at 5pm Eastern. The primary purpose of the group Unity & Belonging is to follow the guideline of Tradition 5. We do this by providing a supportive space for individuals seeking recovery from alcoholism, adhering to the AA Principles, and ensuring the AA traditions guide the decisions of the group.


Meeting Schedule:
- Sun: Big Book Discussion
- Mon: Living Sober Discussion
- Tue: AA Grapevine Discussion
- Wed: Meditation/Daily Reflection
- Thur: Chair’s Choice
- Fri: Speaker Traditions Discussion
- Sat: Speaker Step Discussion
